summary: Plan addresses the right accessibility and consistency problems, but it mixes public-impact work with a draft-only page, and several steps need tighter accessibility, responsive, and rollback safeguards before approval.

issues.description: S4 targets `src/content/docs/index.mdx`, but that page is marked `draft: true`, so the landing-page deduplication will not affect the shipped public site unless the draft status changes.
issues.suggestion: Confirm whether this is intended as a dev-only preview fix or move the change to a published route; otherwise it should not be counted as public-facing remediation.

issues.description: S3 introduces native `<details>` disclosure without a concrete accessibility/styling plan. Default disclosure markers, focus states, and summary spacing may clash with the current card layout and make the section harder to scan.
issues.suggestion: Specify the summary markup, keyboard/focus styling, and open/closed states in CSS, then validate with keyboard navigation and a screen reader pass.

issues.description: S1/S2 change shared tokens (`--loom-soft`, `--loom-card`, radius values) that are reused across many surfaces, but the plan only names a few sample contrasts. That leaves the risk of unintended regressions elsewhere in the site.
issues.suggestion: Add a token-usage audit or a broader visual regression checklist covering every component that consumes the changed tokens, not just the examples listed.

issues.description: The rollback strategy is fragile: it assumes one step equals one clean commit and uses manual tags plus `git revert HEAD`, but several steps touch the same CSS file, so revert boundaries may not stay isolated.
issues.suggestion: Require one commit per step or define explicit checkpoints/branches so each change can be reverted independently without depending on exact commit ordering.
